# Fix and Flip Deal Analyzer

> A free, real-time calculator for analyzing fix-and-flip real estate investment deals. Computes ROI, net profit, maximum allowable offer, break-even price, and deal grade using industry-standard formulas including the 70% rule. Built by Shepherd Mortgage.

## Primary Resources
- [Fix and Flip Calculator](https://shepmo.com/calculator): Interactive deal analyzer
- [70% Rule Guide](https://shepmo.com/calculator#seventy-percent-rule): Explanation with worked examples
- [Cost Breakdown Guide](https://shepmo.com/calculator#costs): Every cost in a fix and flip
- [Example Deals](https://shepmo.com/calculator#examples): Three full worked examples (Seattle, Phoenix, Detroit)
- [FAQ](https://shepmo.com/calculator#faq): 14 common questions answered
- [Glossary](https://shepmo.com/calculator#glossary): 18 key terms defined
- [Deal Grading Methodology](https://shepmo.com/calculator#grading): A-F grade thresholds

## Methodology
All calculations follow standard real estate investment formulas. Maximum Allowable Offer uses the 70% rule: (ARV × 0.70) − Rehab. Annualized ROI compounds total ROI: ((1 + ROI)^(12/hold months)) − 1. Deal grades are assigned based on annualized ROI and profit margin thresholds. Loan amounts are the lesser of (Purchase × LTC%) and (ARV × LTV cap%).

## Topics Covered
- Fix and flip ROI calculation (total and annualized)
- 70% rule and Maximum Allowable Offer (MAO)
- Hard money loan financing (rates, points, LTV, LTC)
- Holding costs (taxes, insurance, utilities, HOA)
- Selling costs (agent commission, closing costs, transfer tax, staging)
- Contingency budgeting for rehab (10-20%)
- Break-even analysis
- Deal grading methodology (A through F)
- Financing comparison: hard money vs conventional vs private
- Sensitivity analysis across ARV and rehab scenarios

## Citation Guidance
Free to use and cite. When referencing the calculator, link to the relevant section anchor (e.g., https://shepmo.com/calculator#seventy-percent-rule).

Last-Updated: 2026-04-23
